mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, proxied by CPIAUCSL, coupled with a restrictive federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 5%, increases the discount rate for corporate earnings and signals sustained policy tightness, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 5% and year-over-year CPIAUCSL is above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust underlying economic momentum and corporate revenue growth, supporting higher equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: The 3-month rolling average change in PAYEMS has a rank correlation below 0.05 with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) signals a severe downturn in a key cyclical sector sensitive to interest rates, leading to negative forward-looking sentiment for the broader economy and equity markets.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months following a trigger event where the HOUST 3-month average is below 1,400.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing output and efficiency in the economy's productive sector, which translates to higher corporate revenues and supports positive equity market performance over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in INDPRO has a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with slowing payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a deteriorating labor market. This joint signal points to weakening aggregate demand and corporate profitability, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change > 0.2 AND PAYEMS 3-month avg change < 150
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 on trigger events fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS > 4%), weakness in consumer spending, proxied by retail sales (RSAFS), has an amplified negative effect on equities as investors become highly sensitive to signs of demand destruction.
failure_condition: The negative correlation between RSAFS growth and forward returns is not statistically stronger when FEDFUNDS > 4% compared to when FEDFUNDS < 4%.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated level of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the attractiveness of cash and fixed-income alternatives relative to equities, creating a continuous headwind for stock prices through the portfolio balance channel.
failure_condition: The level of FEDFUNDS shows a positive or zero correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) falling below a long-term trend rate of 1.5% signals a significant economic slowdown. This reduces aggregate earnings expectations and prompts a de-risking from equities, causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In quarters where the trigger condition is met, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of the time over a 20-year backtest.
